machinery_genes.quote: the secretion of EvpP was dependent on 13 EVP proteins including EvpC (a homologue of Hcp) and EvpI
machinery_genes.note: EvpC = Hcp tube protein of the E. tarda/E. piscicida EVP T6SS. Crystal structure (Jobichen et al 2010, PMID 20886112) confirms an Hcp1-like beta-barrel that stacks into hexameric nanotubes ('shares high sequence similarity with Hcp1'). Hcp counts as machinery per the benchmark brief.

machinery_genes.role: secreted trimeric spike (tube tip / membrane puncturing)
machinery_genes.quote: we identified the entire EVP cluster as a T6SS and two additional secreted proteins (EvpI, a homologue of VgrG, and EvpP) were found
machinery_genes.note: EvpI = VgrG spike. Re-confirmed in the EvpQ paper (PMID 33776977): 'a torch-like VgrG trimer (encoded by evpI)'. VgrG counts as machinery per the brief.

machinery_genes.quote: it consists of a torch-like VgrG trimer (encoded by evpI in E. piscicida) on which sits a PAAR protein sharpening the VgrG tip (encoded by evpJ in E. piscicida), and VgrG itself sits on the Hcp tube (encoded by evpC in E. piscicida)
machinery_genes.note: EvpJ = PAAR tip per the E. piscicida EvpQ paper. PAAR counts as machinery per the brief. Low-confidence/discrepancy flag: the original dissection (Zheng & Leung 2007, PMID 17986187) found EvpJ NOT required for EvpP secretion and grouped it with EvpD as dispensable; later work (this paper) assigns it the PAAR structural role.

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane complex component; ATPase that energizes substrate transport (Walker A/B)
machinery_genes.quote: the presumably reduced ATPase activity conferred by the Walker A mutants of Edwardsiella TssM (EvpO) may remain sufficient for Hcp secretion
machinery_genes.note: EvpO is explicitly equated with the IcmF-family inner-membrane protein TssM. The EvpQ paper (PMID 33776977) states 'EvpO is an ATPase that energizes the transportation of T6SS substrates.' This is the IcmF/TssM membrane-complex energizer, not a separate ClpV/TssH. Zheng & Leung 2007 first described EvpO as 'a putative ATPase which contained a Walker A motif'.

machinery_genes.role: intracellular apparatus protein; interacts with the TssM/IcmF ATPase EvpO
machinery_genes.quote: EvpO, a putative ATPase which contained a Walker A motif, showed possible interactions with three EVP proteins (EvpA, EvpL and EvpN)
machinery_genes.note: Named as a non-secreted intracellular apparatus protein of the EVP T6SS that interacts with the membrane/ATPase component EvpO. The 2007 dissection paper does not assign EvpA to a specific Tss family, so family is left unresolved; low confidence on exact role.

machinery_genes.role: intracellular apparatus protein; interacts with the TssM/IcmF ATPase EvpO
machinery_genes.quote: EvpO, a putative ATPase which contained a Walker A motif, showed possible interactions with three EVP proteins (EvpA, EvpL and EvpN)
machinery_genes.note: Named as a non-secreted intracellular apparatus protein of the EVP T6SS interacting with EvpO. Family not assigned in the primary paper; low confidence on exact role.

machinery_genes.role: intracellular apparatus protein; interacts with the TssM/IcmF ATPase EvpO
machinery_genes.quote: EvpO, a putative ATPase which contained a Walker A motif, showed possible interactions with three EVP proteins (EvpA, EvpL and EvpN)
machinery_genes.note: Named as a non-secreted intracellular apparatus protein of the EVP T6SS interacting with the membrane/ATPase component EvpO. Family not assigned in the primary paper (an E. ictaluri study, Kalindamar et al 2023, PMID 38153949, maps evpN to tssL, but that strain/text could not be quoted verbatim here, so the family is left unresolved). Low confidence.

machinery_genes.role: EVP T6SS cluster protein (dispensable for EvpP secretion)
machinery_genes.quote: We systematically mutagenized all the 16 EVP genes and found that the secretion of EvpP was dependent on 13 EVP proteins including EvpC (a homologue of Hcp) and EvpI but not EvpD and EvpJ
machinery_genes.note: Named as one of the 16 EVP (T6SS) cluster genes. Distinct from the others, EvpD is NOT required for T6SS-dependent secretion and its deletion was not attenuated in fish; included as a named cluster component but flagged lowest confidence as machinery (it may be accessory/non-essential). Family unresolved.

notes: System: the Edwardsiella virulent protein (EVP) gene cluster = the single core T6SS of Edwardsiella tarda/piscicida, a 16-ORF operon (EvpA-EvpP) dissected gene-by-gene by Zheng & Leung 2007 in the fish isolate E. tarda PPD130/91 (now reclassified as E. piscicida; same species/system as EIB202). Strain transfer: the apparatus characterisation used PPD130/91, not EIB202 directly, but they are the same species and EVP T6SS family; component naming transfers. MGE note: this instance's curated effector ETAE_2037 = EvpQ (Li et al 2021, PMID 33776977), a cysteine-protease effector encoded on a horizontally-acquired genomic island (low-GC, tRNA + IS903 transposase flanked), 513 genes from the core T6SS cluster. EvpQ is secreted IN A T6SS-DEPENDENT MANNER by the core EVP apparatus ('the secretion of EvpQ was dependent totally on T6SS'), so the answer-key machinery is the core EVP cluster listed above, NOT MGE-local genes; the only apparatus-related gene reported on the EvpQ island itself is a TssA homolog ('Besides, TssA was also encoded by the same the GI'), which is an effector-loading accessory and is not added here as a separate confirmed machinery entry. Confidently mapped components with verbatim quotes: EvpC=Hcp(TssD), EvpI=VgrG(TssI), EvpJ=PAAR, EvpO=TssM/IcmF ATPase. EvpA/EvpL/EvpN are named as intracellular apparatus proteins (EvpO interactors) but the primary paper assigns no Tss family, so families are unresolved; EvpD is a named but dispensable cluster gene (lowest confidence). Status PARTIAL: the remaining EVP genes (EvpB sheath, EvpE/F/G baseplate, EvpH, EvpK=TssA, EvpM) exist in the operon and are mappable in a secondary E. ictaluri study (Kalindamar et al 2023, PMID 38153949: evpH=tssH/ClpV, evpF=tssF, evpG=tssG, evpK=tssA, evpB=sheath, evpN=tssL, evpO=tssM), but that paper is a different strain and its full text could not be quoted verbatim per gene, so those genes are deliberately omitted rather than padded. Deliberately EXCLUDED: EvpP and EvpQ/ETAE_2037 (delivered effectors), regulators (EsrB/EsrC/Fur/PhoP/RpoS), and the secretion-substrate biology. All quotes verbatim from the cited articles.
